cross_book_pathways.title: Material/Immaterial across the vault
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway placing Hayles's recovery of embodiment beside Han's diagnosis of information. Useful for the vault's central Material/Immaterial axis.

- [[The Body as Data]] — the body recoded as downloadable informational pattern
- [[Embodiment]] — the instantiated, non-replicable particular against 'the body' as abstraction
- [[Material vs Immaterial]] — the vault's master axis
- [[N. Katherine Hayles and Byung-Chul Han]] — two distinct senses of 'information' on the embodiment axis
- [[Information]] — where the two diagnoses meet and diverge

cross_book_pathways.title: Material/Immaterial across art history and cybernetics
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway bridging Lippard's dematerialization of the art object to Hayles's disembodiment of information on the vault's central axis. Useful for reading the Material/Immaterial tension across three authors.

- [[Material vs Immaterial]] — the vault's master axis, now spanning Han, Hayles, and Lippard
- [[Dematerialization]] — the art-historical instance of the immaterial
- [[Lucy Lippard and N. Katherine Hayles]] — dematerialization of the art object ↔ disembodiment of information
- [[The Materiality of Informatics]] — the counter-thesis that the immaterial is materially produced

cross_book_pathways.title: Immaterial labour across the vault (Han ↔ Hayles ↔ Lippard)
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway placing Han's neoliberal immaterial production beside Hayles's disembodiment of information and Lippard's dematerialized art object, on the vault's central Material/Immaterial axis. Useful for reading the material/immaterial tension across philosophy, cybernetics, and art history.

- [[Immaterial Labour]] — neoliberal immaterial production that releases the body from production
- [[Material vs Immaterial]] — the vault's master axis, now spanning four author sections
- [[The Body as Data]] — the body recoded as data; the digital habitus as a likeness of the soul
- [[N. Katherine Hayles and Byung-Chul Han]] — two distinct senses of 'information' on the embodiment axis

cross_book_pathways.title: Material/Immaterial across the vault (Harari)
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway placing Harari's intersubjective fictions and freedom of information beside the body recoded as data, on the vault's central Material/Immaterial axis. Useful for reading the immaterial as the order of fictions more powerful than material things.

- [[Material vs Immaterial]] — the vault's master axis
- [[Intersubjective Reality]] — money, gods, nations: immaterial fictions more powerful than material things
- [[The Body as Data]] — the body recoded as informational pattern
- [[Freedom of Information]] — Dataism's supreme value, prizing flow over substrate
- [[Yuval Noah Harari and N. Katherine Hayles]] — the species recast as a data-processing system

cross_book_pathways.title: Acceleration, two valences
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway juxtaposing Kurzweil's celebration of exponential progress with Han's critique of speed stripped of sense — one of the vault's most productive contrasts for the material/immaterial theme. Useful for thinking what 'acceleration' affirms and what it costs.

- [[The Law of Accelerating Returns]] — Kurzweil's celebration of double-exponential progress
- [[Exponential vs Linear]] — the epistemic axis the whole Singularity forecast hangs on
- [[Paradigm Shift and the S-Curve]] — the exponential as a cascade of S-curves
- [[The Lily Pads on the Lake]] — the parable of exponential onset
- [[Acceleration]] — Han's critique of speed stripped of sense, narrative, and the Other
- [[Narration vs Addition]] — the deceleration counter-figure to additive accumulation

cross_book_pathways.title: The informatics of domination across the vault
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway following Haraway's coded command-control grid and its material substrate into Hayles's pattern thesis, Harari's organisms-as-algorithms, and Han's immaterial labour. Useful for the vault's Material/Immaterial axis and the world recoded as information.

- [[The Informatics of Domination]] — the shift from organic hierarchical dominations to coded control networks
- [[The World as a Problem of Coding]] — the common move of communications sciences and modern biologies
- [[The world is being translated into a problem of coding]] — the argument in Haraway's own voice
- [[Informatics]] — Hayles's term taken from Haraway; the network of information technologies
- [[Pattern and Randomness]] — the master dialectic the coding move feeds into (Hayles)
- [[Organisms Are Algorithms]] — Harari's keystone biological version of the coding thesis
- [[The Homework Economy]] — the feminization of work; the material substrate of the informatics of domination
- [[Women in the Integrated Circuit]] — women's dispersed positions across the microelectronic world-system
- [[Immaterial Labour]] — Han's neoliberal immaterial production; the downstream of the homework economy
- [[Material vs Immaterial]] — the vault's master axis, now spanning six author sections
- [[N. Katherine Hayles and Donna Haraway]] — the cross-book bridge on informatics and the cyborg

cross_book_pathways.title: The electric reversal — explosion into implosion across the vault
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway following McLuhan's master historical claim — mechanical explosion contracting into electric implosion and the global village — into the vault's information, acceleration, and material/immaterial threads. Useful for reading media speed against Han, Hayles, Harari, and Kurzweil.

- [[Explosion vs Implosion]] — mechanical explosion reversing into electric implosion
- [[The Global Village]] — electric speed contracting the planet to a single participatory community
- [[Transportation as Communication]] — physical transport and information movement as one continuous 'communication'
- [[Money as a Medium]] — money's arc from commodity to credit-information
- [[The Credit Card]] — money as pure credit-information; the electric reversal toward 'tribal money'
- [[Material vs Immaterial]] — the vault's master axis, now spanning seven author sections
- [[Information]] — where McLuhan's electric-information thread meets Hayles, Lippard, and Harari
- [[Acceleration]] — Han's critique of speed, the productive contrast to McLuhan's 'electric speed'
- [[Marshall McLuhan and Byung-Chul Han]] — electric speed and the leased nervous system read against Han
- [[Marshall McLuhan and N. Katherine Hayles]] — the senses as substrate and consciousness translated into information

cross_book_pathways.title: Attention and the senses (a bridge to McLuhan)
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway placing Han's attention-regime diagnosis beside McLuhan's account of media reshaping the ratios of perception. Useful for thinking deep vs scattered attention, multitasking, and perception beneath opinion.

- [[Deep Contemplative Attention]] — the immersive lingering attention that is the precondition of culture
- [[Hyperattention]] — the scattered, broad-but-flat attention-regime that displaces deep reflection
- [[Multitasking]] — regression to the divided vigilance of wild animals, not progress
- [[Deep Attention vs Hyperattention]] — the attention-regime opposition
- [[Sense Ratios]] — McLuhan: media alter the ratios and patterns of perception beneath opinion
- [[Marshall McLuhan and Byung-Chul Han]] — the media-ecology precursor to Han's attention diagnosis

cross_book_pathways.title: The dematerialization lineage across the vault (Burnham ↔ Lippard ↔ Hayles)
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway placing Burnham's dematerialization of sculpture beside Lippard's dematerialization of the art object and Hayles's disembodiment of information, on the vault's central Material/Immaterial axis. Useful for reading dematerialization across art history and cybernetics.

- [[Material vs Immaterial]] — the vault's master axis, now spanning eight author sections
- [[Object and System]] — Burnham's art-historical instance of the axis
- [[Dematerialization]] — Lippard's titular concept; the conceptual-art instance
- [[A Scribble in the Air]] — Read via Burnham: the dematerialization of postwar sculpture
- [[Embodiment]] — Hayles's instantiated particular against the disembodiment of information
- [[Jack Burnham and Lucy Lippard]] — the keystone dematerialization / object-to-system bridge
- [[Jack Burnham and N. Katherine Hayles]] — cybernetics and systems as a precursor to the posthuman body

cross_book_pathways.title: The Burnham ↔ Ascott cybernetic-art pairing
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway pairing the vault's two great theorists of cybernetic/systems art, from Burnham's object-to-system migration into Ascott's network and post-biological telos. Useful for reading the art/systems-esthetics lineage and the object → system → network spine.

- [[Object and System]] — Burnham's structural axis; the art-historical instance of the Material/Immaterial axis
- [[Cyborg Art]] — Burnham's 'cybernetic organism as an art form'; the vault's earliest cyborg (1968)
- [[Behaviourist Art]] — Ascott's contemporaneous behavioural-systems programme
- [[Art as a Form of Behaviour]] — the reversal of 'the behaviour of forms' into art as a mode of behaviour
- [[Network as Artwork]] — the migration's endpoint: the network, not the object, as the work
- [[The Post-Biological Body]] — the body transformed beyond its biological limits
- [[Roy Ascott and Jack Burnham]] — the keystone art-kin bridge between the two cybernetic-art theorists
- [[Material vs Immaterial]] — the vault's master axis, now spanning nine author sections

cross_book_pathways.title: The surveillance arc across the vault (Han → Harari → Zuboff)
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway following the vault's surveillance thread from Han's diagnosis of the friendly, voluntary digital panopticon, through Harari's Dataism and the recasting of the human as a data source, to Zuboff's full anatomy of surveillance capitalism and Big Other — with Kurzweil and Ascott as the embracing counterpoint that affirms the merger the others diagnose. Useful for reading surveillance, prediction, and the future of the self across philosophy, history, and political economy.

- [[Digital Panopticon]] — Han's friendly, internal panopticon running on voluntary self-exposure
- [[Friendly Big Brother]] — Han's benevolent-faced watcher who is loved, not feared
- [[Big Data]] — Han's aperspectival instrument that pre-empts free will
- [[Dataism]] — Harari's data-religion that would retire humanity
- [[Organisms Are Algorithms]] — Harari's keystone thesis recasting the human as a data source
- [[Surveillance Capitalism]] — Zuboff's economic logic that claims human experience as raw material
- [[Big Other]] — Zuboff's ubiquitous computational architecture, the successor to the panopticon
- [[Instrumentarian Power]] — Zuboff's new species of power that knows and shapes behavior
- [[Big Brother vs Big Other]] — the tension that distinguishes Zuboff's apparatus from totalitarian terror
- [[The Singularity]] — Kurzweil's embraced merger of human and machine intelligence; the affirmative counterpoint
- [[Network Consciousness]] — Ascott's affirmative vision of the planetary networked mind
- [[Shoshana Zuboff and Byung-Chul Han]] — the keystone surveillance kin-pairing
- [[Shoshana Zuboff and Yuval Noah Harari]] — rendition beside Dataism
- [[Shoshana Zuboff and Ray Kurzweil]] — the critical-vs-affirmative pole of the data future

cross_book_pathways.title: Material/Immaterial across the vault (Zuboff)
cross_book_pathways.body:
A cross-book pathway placing Zuboff's rendition of lived experience into behavioral data, and human experience claimed as the fourth fictional commodity, on the vault's central Material/Immaterial axis — the tenth and final author section. Useful for reading the immaterial as the extracted, commodified residue of material life.

- [[Material vs Immaterial]] — the vault's master axis, now spanning ten author sections
- [[Rendition]] — the operations that translate lived experience into behavioral data
- [[The Fourth Fictional Commodity]] — human experience claimed as a commodity, after land, labor, and money
- [[Behavioral Surplus]] — the immaterial surplus extracted from material life
- [[The Body as Data]] — the body recoded as informational pattern; the thread Zuboff's rendition extends
- [[Shoshana Zuboff and Byung-Chul Han]] — Big Other and rendition beside the digital panopticon

gloss_tensions.Visibility vs Understanding: — hyperinformation does not converge on truth (realized via Han)
gloss_tensions.Embodiment vs Information: — disembodied informational pattern vs enacted, instantiated embodiment (realized via Hayles)
gloss_tensions.Transparency vs Opacity: — Han's core opposition across eros, hermeneutics, the holy (realized via Han)
gloss_tensions.Freedom vs Control: — the dialectic of freedom in the digital panopticon; now extended by Psychopolitics — freedom itself producing coercion (realized via Han)
gloss_tensions.System vs Subject: — now realized via Han's [[Subject vs Project]]: the cast-down subject vs the self-fashioning project
gloss_tensions.Subject vs Project: — the cast-down subject vs the self-fashioning project that re-subjugates itself (Han, Psychopolitics)
gloss_tensions.Desire vs Danger: — eros vs pornography; veil vs total exposure (realized via Han)
gloss_tensions.Material vs Immaterial: — now realized across ten author sections: Han (transparency/information; immaterial labour), Hayles, Lippard's art-historical dematerialization, O'Gieblyn, Kurzweil, Haraway (the cyborg as a condensed image of both imagination and material reality), McLuhan (money's commodity→information arc; mechanical explosion vs electric implosion), Burnham (sculpture's migration from object to system; the [[Object vs System]] axis), Ascott (cyberception making immaterial process palpable; the rematerialization of the body by networking; the prosthesis of vision dissolving the material/immaterial divide), and Zuboff (the rendition of lived experience into behavioral data; human experience claimed as the fourth fictional commodity)
gloss_tensions.Object vs System: — Burnham's framing of the canonical [[Material vs Immaterial]] axis; the bounded sculptural object vs the system of forces/energy/information (also gathers Matter vs Energy) (Burnham, Beyond Modern Sculpture)
gloss_tensions.Positivity vs Negativity: — the master conceptual pair across two Han books; positivity vs negativity as a theory of power (Psychopolitics) and as the pathology/diagnosis register of excess positivity (The Burnout Society)
gloss_tensions.Narration vs Addition: — narrative gravity vs additive accumulation; counting is not recounting (Psychopolitics)
gloss_tensions.Biopolitics vs Psychopolitics: — power over the body and population vs power over the psyche (Han, Psychopolitics)
gloss_tensions.Discipline vs Control: — confinement and the mole vs modulation and the snake (Han via Deleuze, Psychopolitics)
gloss_tensions.Counting vs Recounting: — additive data vs narrative meaning (Han, Psychopolitics)
gloss_tensions.Vita Activa vs Vita Contemplativa: — the book-defining opposition: hyperactive laboring vs the contemplative life as sovereign saying-no; the Action-vs-Laboring distinction folded inside (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_tensions.Deep Attention vs Hyperattention: — the attention-regime opposition: immersive lingering attention vs the scattered, broad-but-flat regime of multitasking (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_tensions.I-Tiredness vs We-Tiredness: — the exhausting, isolating tiredness vs the healing, reconciling tiredness; the two poles of Fundamental Tiredness (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_tensions.Discipline vs Achievement: — confinement, prohibition, and the should vs auto-exploitation, permission, and the can; realizes the dangling author-note link, distinct from Discipline vs Control (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_tensions.Positive Potency vs Negative Potency: — the faculty-level pair: the power to do vs the power not to do (the power to say no); distinct from Positivity vs Negativity (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_tensions.Presence Absence vs Pattern Randomness: — the epistemic shift displacing the Western presence/absence dialectic with pattern/randomness (Hayles)
gloss_tensions.Liberal Humanist Subject vs Posthuman: — autonomous self-possession vs distributed-cognition amalgam; lethal only when one is grafted onto the other (Hayles)
gloss_tensions.Homeostasis vs Reflexivity: — man as self-regulating mechanism vs man spliced into a reflexive informational circuit (the Macy contest) (Hayles)
gloss_tensions.Disenchantment vs Re-enchantment: — the meaningless mechanistic cosmos vs. the persistent drive to re-enchant it by scientific means; O'Gieblyn's master axis (transhumanism, the re-enchanted network, panpsychism)
gloss_tensions.Free Will vs Determinism: — spontaneous volition vs. the deterministic algorithm/predestination; foresight-vs-freedom, prediction-vs-control (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_tensions.Metaphor vs Literalism: — the acknowledged analogy vs. its literalization ('the universe is like a computer' vs. 'is in fact a computer') (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_tensions.Subject vs Object: — the irreducible first-person vantage vs. the god's-eye 'view from nowhere' (Bohr vs. the multiverse) (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_tensions.Human Exceptionalism vs Ontological Equa: — privileging the human subject (imago dei) vs. dethroning it (deep ecology, the human as virus/glitch/error) (O'Gieblyn); Haraway author-section added — the affirmatively breached human/animal boundary
gloss_tensions.Intelligence vs Consciousness: — the decoupling opposition; the book's closing question of whether intelligence can sever from awareness (Harari)
gloss_tensions.Meaning vs Power: — the structuring opposition of the modern covenant: modernity trades meaning for power (Harari)
gloss_tensions.Objective vs Subjective vs Intersubjecti: — Harari's three-pole ontology; the intersubjective fictions more powerful than material things (Harari)
gloss_tensions.Healing vs Upgrading: — egalitarian medicine that heals the sick vs elitist medicine that upgrades the healthy into biological castes (Harari)
gloss_tensions.Religion vs Spirituality: — religion as a deal that legitimates a social order vs spirituality as an open-ended journey (Harari)
gloss_tensions.Truth vs Cooperative Power: — accurate world views vs the fictions that enable large-scale cooperation; truth is not what gives sapiens mastery (Harari)
gloss_tensions.Distributed vs Centralised Processing: — the Cold War reread as a contest of data architectures (capitalism vs communism) (Harari)
gloss_tensions.Homo-Centric vs Data-Centric: — humanism's worship of human experience vs Dataism's worship of information flow (Harari)
gloss_tensions.Exponential vs Linear: — the historically-grounded exponential reality of accelerating returns vs the intuitive-linear perception that blinds forecasters to it; the epistemic axis the whole Singularity forecast hangs on (Kurzweil)
gloss_tensions.Biology vs Technology: — slow, frail, fixed-capacity 'version 1.0' biological intelligence vs fast, designable, double-exponential nonbiological intelligence that predominates yet stays 'human' (Kurzweil)
gloss_tensions.Domination vs Possibility: — Haraway's double vision: the cyborg world as a grid of control AND the ground of emancipatory kinship, held without synthesis (Haraway)
gloss_tensions.Cyborg vs Goddess: — the technological/ironic/partial cyborg against the organic/holistic/reproductive goddess; 'I would rather be a cyborg than a goddess' (Haraway)
gloss_tensions.Regeneration vs Rebirth: — monstrous, potent reconstitution after injury (the salamander) against the reproductive return to wholeness (Haraway)
gloss_tensions.Mechanical vs Electric: — McLuhan's master historical opposition: fragmenting mechanical explosion vs contracting electric implosion (McLuhan, Understanding Media)
gloss_tensions.Fragmentation vs Wholeness: — the literate/print self vs the electric integral self (McLuhan)
gloss_tensions.Detachment vs Participation: — literate noninvolvement vs electric depth participation; print detachment vs cool-media participation (McLuhan)
gloss_tensions.Hot vs Cool Media: — the hot form excludes and detribalizes, the cool form includes and retribalizes (McLuhan)
gloss_tensions.Numbness vs Awareness: — the necessary narcosis of extension against the awareness that breaks the media-trance (McLuhan)
gloss_tensions.Liberty vs Capture: — the self's rights over its senses against the leased, corporately manipulated nervous system (McLuhan)
gloss_tensions.Center vs Margin: — centralization vs decentralization read off media speed; electric speed dissolves the margin (McLuhan)
gloss_tensions.Job vs Role: — the delegated specialist job of mechanical/print culture vs the collective nonvisual role restored by electric media (McLuhan)
gloss_tensions.Mechanism vs Vitalism: — the centuries-long biological dispute carried into sculpture (Burnham)
gloss_tensions.Organic vs Geometric: — the Worringer/Hauser polarity dissolved in vitalist art and fusible by technology (Burnham)
gloss_tensions.Vitalism vs Organicism: — poetic life-essence vs the utilitarian evolution of systems (Burnham)
gloss_tensions.Gravity vs Mobility: — the base as anchorage vs the will to flight/levitation (Burnham, Ch1)
gloss_tensions.Form vs Light: — solid form/mass vs surface optics and light as the determinant of perception (Burnham)
gloss_tensions.Idealism vs Phenomenalism: — transcendent idealism (icon/object) vs transcendent phenomenalism (perceptual situation); folds Object vs Experience (Burnham)
gloss_tensions.Apollonian vs Faustian: — static sculpture's quietude vs automata's drive to transcend natural limits (Spengler via Burnham)
gloss_tensions.Anthropomorphic vs Non-Anthropomorphic L: — copying human form vs modeling thought (Pascal/Leibniz, pseudo-organisms) (Burnham)
gloss_tensions.Art vs Technics: — irrationality/madness vs the self-augmenting ethos of technics (Burnham, Ch6)
gloss_tensions.One-Way Stimulation vs Two-Way Communica: — the cybernetic criterion separating sculpture from system (Burnham, Ch8)
gloss_tensions.Living vs Nonliving Matter: — the boundary Burnham collapses into a scale of complexity (Burnham, Ch8)
gloss_tensions.Objecthood vs Theatre: — Burnham's inversion of Fried toward a systems perspective (Burnham, Ch8)
gloss_tensions.Process vs Product: — the behaviourist/telematic core opposition: art as ongoing process vs the finished object (Ascott, Telematic Embrace)
gloss_tensions.Connection vs Narcissism: — the telematic embrace as loving connection vs the closed, mirror-bound self; 'Is there love in the telematic embrace?' (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Liberation vs Control (Telematics): — the network's emancipatory potential vs its capture by corporate and military power (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Cyb vs Psi: — the cybernetic and the psychic/parapsychological poles fused in psibernetics (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Horizontal vs Vertical: — the decentralized, lateral network vs the hierarchical, top-down structure (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Isolation vs Convergence: — the disconnected individual vs the convergent, connective field of network consciousness (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Creativity vs Production: — generative, emergent creativity vs the logic of industrial production (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Representation vs Simulation: — picturing the world vs modelling/making it; the turn telematic art performs (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Observer vs Participator: — the detached spectator vs the active co-producer of the work (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Absorption vs Immersion: — passive absorption in an image vs active immersion in a system (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Verisimilitude vs Modeling: — lifelike resemblance vs the construction of a model/world (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Telenoia vs Paranoia: — networked empathy/mind-at-a-distance vs the closed paranoid self (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Appearance vs Apparition: — representation/appearance vs the coming-into-being of the new (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Surface and Structure vs Living System: — the static formalist object vs the dynamic living system (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Bottom-up vs Top-down: — emergent, artificial-life processes vs imposed, hierarchical design (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Individual vs Interface: — the unified individual vs the distributed self constituted at the interface (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Natural vs Artificial: — the dichotomy dissolved in Nature II, where the most artificial returns us to a new nature (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Art vs Commerce: — the creative network vs its colonisation by the market (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Representation vs Virtualisation: — depicting the given vs generating the virtual (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Reductionism vs Holism: — the analytic part vs the connective whole; networking as the antithesis of reductionism (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Explanation vs Transformation: — explaining consciousness vs transforming it; the artist's transformative vocation (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Performance vs Enactment: — staged performance vs the participatory enactment of meaning (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Dry vs Wet vs Moist: — the digital (dry) and biological (wet) converging into the third, moist domain (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Being vs Becoming: — fixed being vs the processual becoming of the post-biological self (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Trivial vs Nontrivial Interactivity: — predetermined response vs genuinely generative, open interaction (Ascott)
gloss_tensions.Knowledge vs Power: — the radical asymmetry: surveillance capital knows everything about us while we know almost nothing about it (Zuboff, The Age of Surveillance Capitalism)
gloss_tensions.Instrumentarian Power vs Totalitarianism: — the new species of power that knows and shapes behavior to others' ends, distinct from the violent ideological state (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Big Brother vs Big Other: — the central periodizing distinction: the totalitarian state's violent terror vs the ubiquitous, indifferent computational architecture that renders and modifies behavior; cross-links the symbol [[Friendly Big Brother]] (Han) and the concept [[Big Other]] (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Home vs Exile: — dwelling in inviolable sanctuary vs the homelessness of glass life (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Inevitability vs Contingency: — the ideological fraud of autonomous technological destiny vs the human authorship of the future (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.The Individual vs The Hive: — the autonomous self vs the confluent, totally connected collective instrumentarianism engineers (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Empowerment vs Diminishment: — the promise of the digital advocacy vs the dispossession surveillance capitalism delivered (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Individualization vs Individualism: — the second-modern social fact of the self-determining individual vs the market ideology of individualism (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Production vs Extraction: — the constructive reciprocities of classical capitalism vs the one-way extraction of behavioral surplus (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Privacy vs Decision Rights: — privacy reframed not as a thing eroded but as decision rights redistributed to surveillance capital (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Transparency of the Watched vs Secrecy o: — the glass life of the user vs the structural hiding strategy of the apparatus (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Freedom vs Democracy: — the neoliberal elevation of market freedom against the claims of collective self-rule (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Citizen vs User: — the bearer of political rights vs the rendered subject of the apparatus (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Division of Labor vs Division of Learnin: — the old axial principle of industrial society vs the new axial principle of who knows, who decides, who decides who decides (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Determinism vs Indeterminacy: — guaranteed outcomes engineered through actuation vs the open, indeterminate future the will to will requires (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Consent vs Coercion: — manufactured agreement via contracts of adhesion vs the coercion of the uncontract; consent is coercion (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Awareness vs Evasion: — human consciousness as friction to be engineered away vs the art and science of hiding (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Certainty vs Uncertainty: — instrumentarianism's utopia of certainty vs the freedom that requires uncertainty (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Autonomy vs Heteronomy: — the self-authored will to will vs behavior steered to others' ends (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Content vs Form: — what we say vs the predictive surplus in how we say it; form predicts more than content (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Soul vs Behavior: — the inner person of meaning vs the behaving object viewed under radical indifference (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Theory vs Practice: — applied utopistics: utopias realized in practice without an explicit, contestable theory (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Sanctuary vs Glass Life: — the right to inviolable space vs the totally exposed existence Big Other produces (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Nature vs Human Nature: — industrial capitalism's exploitation of nature vs surveillance capitalism's exploitation of human nature (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Fusion vs Autonomy: — homing to the herd and the death of individuality vs the autonomous self (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Equivalence vs Equality: — the hive's leveling sameness that imposes confluence without equal standing (Zuboff)
gloss_tensions.Market vs Democracy: — the unaccountable sovereign power of behavioral futures markets vs popular sovereignty (Zuboff)

gloss_symbols.Aaron Swartz, Dataism's First Martyr: — the open-access activist as the symbol of the freedom-of-information creed (Harari)
gloss_symbols.August 9, 2011: — Zuboff: the date that crystallizes the rise of surveillance capitalism and the foreclosure of alternatives (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.Ayahuasca, the Vine of the Soul: — Ascott: the shamanic plant technology of consciousness, parallel to the digital web (Ascott)
gloss_symbols.Blasphemy: — Haraway's faithful-but-not-reverent method, paired with irony (Haraway)
gloss_symbols.C3I: — command-control-communication-intelligence as the condensed emblem of the coded-control regime (Haraway)
gloss_symbols.Canned Dreams: — film as the most magical of consumer commodities, the American way of life exported 'in cans' (McLuhan)
gloss_symbols.Friendly Big Brother: — the benevolent-faced watcher who is loved, not feared; the digital panopticon's affable sovereign (Han, Psychopolitics)
gloss_symbols.Google Street View: — Zuboff: the dispossession cycle in action; incursion into public and private space normalized as inevitable (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.Janet Freed: — the erased embodied transcriptionist (Freed/Freud) who shows information is never disembodied (Hayles)
gloss_symbols.Job and the Algorithm: — the Book of Job as the figure of submission to the inscrutable algorithmic sovereign (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_symbols.Science Finds Industry Applies Man Confo: — Zuboff: the 1933 Century of Progress motto as the creed of behavioral conditioning and instrumentarian order (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.Smart Dust: — In Ray Kurzweil's *The Singularity Is Near*, "smart dust" is a real DARPA development program: "complex sensor systems not much bigger than a pinhead." Kurzweil…
gloss_symbols.The 2003 Targeted Advertising Patent: — Zuboff: Google's patent as the documentary origin of the behavioral-surplus logic (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.The Aibo: — the robot dog O'Gieblyn cannot switch off; the book's opening laboratory for machine interiority and the problem of other minds
gloss_symbols.The Aware Home and the Nest Thermostat: — Zuboff: the book's opening case; the smart home that inverts into a node of rendition and dispossession (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.The Bosworth Memo: — Zuboff: the leaked Facebook memo affirming growth at any cost; the candid internal voice of radical indifference (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.The Change Painting: — Ascott: the early reconfigurable, viewer-altered panel painting; the seed-object of behaviourist art (Ascott)
gloss_symbols.The Chinese Room: — Searle's room reread (via Hutchins): the whole room, not Searle, knows Chinese — distributed cognition (Hayles)
gloss_symbols.The Credit Card: — money as pure credit-information; the electric reversal toward 'tribal money' (McLuhan)
gloss_symbols.The Electric Light: — pure information, a medium without a content; the clearest proof that the medium is the message (McLuhan)
gloss_symbols.The Forest of Arden: — Shakespeare's golden world as an advance model of the automation age (McLuhan)
gloss_symbols.The Gestation Crate: — industrial farming as the test of humanist ethics; the sow's suffering as the measure of the modern covenant (Harari)
gloss_symbols.The Hi-Tech Ark: — climate inaction as a class gamble: the elite trusting technology to save them while the masses drown (Harari)
gloss_symbols.The Idol (113th Psalm): — the Hebrew counterpart to Narcissus; we become what we behold (McLuhan)
gloss_symbols.The Large Glass: — Ascott: Duchamp's Bride Stripped Bare reread as a precursor to telematic, observer-completed art (Ascott)
gloss_symbols.The Like Button: — emblem of consensus-positivity; the capitalism of Like, Like as the digital Amen (Psychopolitics)
gloss_symbols.The Mask: — persona, Schein, symbolic appearance transparency wars against
gloss_symbols.The Mole and the Snake: — Deleuze's paired animals: the disciplinary mole of enclosure vs the neoliberal snake of modulation (Han, Psychopolitics)
gloss_symbols.The Montes Bakery: — Zuboff: the Spanish bakery's right-to-be-forgotten case as the assertion of decision rights against dispossession (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.The No-Sculptures (Goodman): — the terminal emblem of the biomorphic vocabulary and Form Exhaustion (Burnham)
gloss_symbols.The Opened Berlin Wall: — Zuboff: the fall of one form of total control as the foil to the rise of a new, frictionless one (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.The Panopticon: — architectural figure of surveillance, now distributed and voluntary
gloss_symbols.The Pylon Sign (Las Vegas): — the illuminated roadside sign as an arch-underground American art form (Burnham)
gloss_symbols.The Remotely Disabled Car: — Zuboff: the uncontract in action; the car that will not start, automated execution voiding human reciprocity (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.The Requirimiento: — Zuboff: the Spanish conquistadors' declaration read aloud to the uncomprehending; the template for conquest by declaration (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.The Robot: — In Burnham's history of automata, the robot is the modern, debased descendant of the human-looking automaton — the clanking folklore figure of what he calls "th…
gloss_symbols.The Scanning Tunnelling Microscope: — Ascott: the instrument that constructs what it images at the molecular scale; the prosthesis of vision (Ascott)
gloss_symbols.The Scientific Model as Icon: — the contemporary icon par excellence; the model as the form-world's sacred image (Burnham)
gloss_symbols.The Table: — Ascott: the diagrammatic table mapping the cybernetic/behaviourist field of art (Ascott)
gloss_symbols.The Tortoise (Machina Speculatrix): — Grey Walter's Elmer & Elsie, the first cybernetic organisms (Burnham, Ch8)
gloss_symbols.The Transparent Customer: — homo sacer of the economic panopticon
gloss_symbols.The Ultimate Laptop: — Lloyd's physical maximum for a 1 kg computer (~5 x 10^50 ops/sec); the symbol of computation's vast physical runway (Kurzweil)
gloss_symbols.Todays Selfie Is Tomorrows Biometric Pro: — Zuboff: the rendition of the face; voluntary self-exposure becoming permanent surveillance data (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.Toys That Spy: — Zuboff: the rendition of childhood; smart toys as instruments of extraction in the home (Zuboff)
gloss_symbols.Vaucansons Duck: — the golden-age automaton reproducing internal organic functions (Burnham)
gloss_symbols.Walden Two and 1984: — Zuboff: Skinner's behaviorist utopia beside Orwell's dystopia; the two poles framing instrumentarian power (Zuboff)

gloss_metaphors.A Game About a Game: — Zuboff: Pokemon Go as a real-world behavioral futures market disguised as play
gloss_metaphors.A Scribble in the Air: — Read via Burnham: the dematerialization of postwar sculpture (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.A Thing That Has Us: — Zuboff: the inversion of mastery; we do not have the apparatus, it has us
gloss_metaphors.All That Is Moist and Alive Must Hand Ov: — Zuboff: the rendition imperative reaching into every living thing
gloss_metaphors.An Eye for an Ear: — the alphabet's trade of the auditory tribal world for the visual civilized one (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Asking a Giraffe to Shorten Its Neck: — Zuboff: expecting surveillance capitalists to self-regulate against their own extraction imperative
gloss_metaphors.Babel vs Pentecost: — language as division (Babel) vs computer-mediated universal understanding (Pentecost) (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Bye-Bye Baudrillard: — Ascott: the dismissal of postmodern simulacral pessimism in favour of constructive participation
gloss_metaphors.Capitalism Should Not Be Eaten Raw: — Zuboff via Polanyi: raw capitalism as antisocial, to be cooked by democratic institutions
gloss_metaphors.Computers Arose from the Mud and Code Fe: — Dyson's dual-creation-myth figure for the dualism behind emergence (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_metaphors.Consciousness Without Walls: — the cosmic endpoint where electric collective consciousness obsolesces housing and language (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Consciousness as Mental Pollution: — consciousness as a useless by-product of brain activity, like the noise of a jet engine (Harari)
gloss_metaphors.Dancing Escapes the Achievement-Principl: — the walking → running → dancing parable: dancing as the leap out of the logic of acceleration and achievement (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_metaphors.Embodying Space Without Matter Like Musi: — the deepest immaterial aspiration of Western technology (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.Every American Home Has Its Berlin Wall: — electric retribalization figured as generational conflict (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Form as a Waterfall Not a Crystal: — Sinnott via Burnham: the organism as dynamic equilibrium (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.Graven Images: — AI as failed imitation of life; 'all we can make are graven images' (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_metaphors.Growbag Culture: — Ascott: culture cultivated and grown like a horticultural medium
gloss_metaphors.How Smart Is a Rock: — a kilogram of matter already computes ~10^42 cps; intelligence as the purposeful organization of computation matter already does (Kurzweil)
gloss_metaphors.Hyperculture as Horticulture: — Ascott: networked hyperculture figured as a cultivated, growing garden
gloss_metaphors.Hypnosis vs Hallucination: — heating one sense hypnotizes, cooling all senses hallucinates (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Indra's Net: — Ascott: the Buddhist net of jewels each reflecting all the others; the holomatic figure of network consciousness
gloss_metaphors.Inevitability as an Existential Narcotic: — Zuboff: inevitabilism as a sedative that numbs resistance to the unprecedented
gloss_metaphors.Information Lost Its Body: — the governing metaphor of Hayles's first story; information as a 'bodiless fluid' between substrates
gloss_metaphors.Iron Filings in the Presence of a Magnet: — Zuboff: benign technology aligning to surveillance capitalism's force field
gloss_metaphors.Joey the Mechanical Boy: — Bettelheim: the human who finds security in becoming a robot (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.Language Does for Intelligence What the: — Bergson's analogy for language as extending yet dividing technology (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Leasing the Nerves (the Archimedean Leve: — the corporate world-mover standing on our leased nerves (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Lightless Radiation: — sourceless suffusion that homogenizes rather than illuminates
gloss_metaphors.Listening with the Entire Epidermis: — the electric-age return to whole-sensorium tactile life (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Machines Made of Sunshine: — the immaterial microelectronic machine as 'nothing but signals'; 'Cyborgs are ether, quintessence' (Haraway)
gloss_metaphors.Make Them Dance: — Zuboff: economies of action figured as choreographing behavior toward guaranteed outcomes
gloss_metaphors.Man as the Sex Organs of the Machine Wor: — man fecundating the machine world as the bee does the plant (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Metonymy (Mind as Microcosm of Cosmic Co: — the mind as microcosm of the world's macroscopic consciousness (panpsychism) (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_metaphors.Mind as Machine: — REALIZED (was a stub): the mind/brain as the era's dominant machine (clock, mill, telegraph, radio, computer); brain = hardware running the software of the mind; slides into literalism (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_metaphors.Narcissus as Narcosis: — the figure of technological numbness, not vanity; the root of Narcissus is narcosis (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.Nature as the First Virtual Reality: — Ascott: nature reread as the original VR, dissolving the natural/artificial divide
gloss_metaphors.Now We Are the Animals: — Zuboff: the animal-telemetry template turned on human beings; we are the herded, tracked creatures
gloss_metaphors.Pollock's Arena as the Epitome of Telema: — Ascott: the action-painting field reread as a precursor to the networked arena
gloss_metaphors.Prometheus and the Eagle: — Kafka's Prometheus reread as self-exploitation: the eagle the chained hero's own exhaustion feeding on itself (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_metaphors.Pygmalion and Galatea: — Ovid: the founding myth of bringing the made figure to life (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.Render Unto Caesar: — Zuboff: rendition as the surrender of experience to the surveillance sovereign
gloss_metaphors.Retiring the Horses: — human obsolescence under Dataism, as cars retired horses from the economy (Harari)
gloss_metaphors.Technology as Magic: — Clarke's third law + incantations-as-algorithms; the anti-disenchantment figure ('technology does not lose its transcendent power when its secrets are revealed') (Kurzweil)
gloss_metaphors.The Abandoned Carcass: — Zuboff: the depleted self left behind once its behavioral surplus has been extracted
gloss_metaphors.The Age of the Iron Whim: — the typewriter-and-typist as agents of female vocation and the homogenizing of business; the 'twelve-pound look' (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Artwork as Matrix and Catalyst: — Ascott: the work not as object but as a generative matrix and catalyst of behaviour and meaning
gloss_metaphors.The Biology of Building vs the Physics o: — Ascott: architecture grown as biology against space conceived as physics
gloss_metaphors.The Body as Congealed Metaphor: — the body as sedimented, non-replicable evolutionary history machines do not share
gloss_metaphors.The Brain Outside the Skull: — the body's interior exteriorized as electric technology (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Brothel-without-Walls: — the photographic age as a wall-less brothel mass-producing the purchasable human image, after Genet (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Bug Was a Feature: — Zuboff: the pattern of privacy violations as structural design, not accident
gloss_metaphors.The Burglar's Meat: — content as the decoy a burglar throws to distract the watchdog of the mind (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Carapace (Car as Shell): — the car as the hard exoskeletal shell of urban man; cars as shiny-backed insects (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Copilot for Life Itself: — Zuboff: the apparatus reframed as an indispensable guide to living, masking dispossession as service
gloss_metaphors.The Crucifix Made of Machine Parts: — faith mechanized; the literal image of theology migrating into engineering (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_metaphors.The Dragon's Teeth (Cadmus): — letters as agents of aggressive martial order, via Canetti (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Dream Bird That Hatches the Egg of E: — Benjamin's figure for deep boredom as the womb of creative experience, killed by hyperattention (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_metaphors.The Earth as a Map of Itself: — total data gathering as the corollary of the outered central nervous system (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Event Horizon of the Singularity: — The historical Singularity as a black hole: a region of "unimaginably large values" bounded by an **event horizon** we cannot see past from outside. Just as the…
gloss_metaphors.The Gift of Gutenberg: — print's exact repeatability transforming the medieval zero into Renaissance infinity (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Glassy Human: — the subject rendered see-through by transparency
gloss_metaphors.The Global Village: — the globe shrunk to a single participatory village by electric speed (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Golem: — the clay android animated by symbols; the pre-modern template for AI and its eschatological charge (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_metaphors.The Gun in Act I: — Chekhov's law framing the book's self-undermining: humanism's logic loaded in the first act fires in the last (Harari)
gloss_metaphors.The Hand and the Glove: — Zuboff: natural psychological need (the hand) engineered into compulsion by the apparatus (the glove)
gloss_metaphors.The Hooded Machine: — sculpture as a black box hiding a supposed inner life force; vitalism applied to the machine (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.The Horseless Carriage: — Zuboff: the new perceived through obsolete categories; the syndrome of failing to see the unprecedented as unprecedented
gloss_metaphors.The Inchworm in the Future Tense: — Zuboff: the apparatus creeping forward to colonize the future and the will to will
gloss_metaphors.The Internal Work Camp: — everyone carries a work camp inside; the achievement-subject as guard and inmate at once (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_metaphors.The Invasive Species That Creates Its Ow: — Zuboff: surveillance capitalism manufacturing the behavioral surplus it feeds on
gloss_metaphors.The Invisible College and the Floating M: — Ascott: the distributed, placeless institution of networked learning and exhibition
gloss_metaphors.The Kybernetes as the New Prime Mover: — the cybernetic steersman replacing the elan vital (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.The Light Bulb as Pure Information: — McLuhan/Piene: art as energy and information (Burnham, Ch7)
gloss_metaphors.The Lily Pads on the Lake: — the parable of exponential onset: the last seven doublings cover the whole lake (Kurzweil)
gloss_metaphors.The Machine Hive Becomes the Human Hive: — Zuboff: the confluence of machines as the template imposed on the human collective
gloss_metaphors.The Machine of Positivity: — the computer / positivized mind as Positivmaschine: a calculating apparatus with no negativity, no hesitation, no no (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_metaphors.The Mechanized Stage as Organism: — Schlemmer: the immersive programmed stage with man as 'the perfect engineer' (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.The Midas Touch: — all technology turning every function into its own form (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Mirror Always Lies: — Ascott: representation/appearance as falsification; the case against the mirror of reflection
gloss_metaphors.The Moat Around the Castle: — Zuboff: the fortifications of secrecy, rhetoric, and politics that protect the new logic from scrutiny
gloss_metaphors.The Music-Box Automata: — Wiener's one-way-message figure for all prior sculpture (Burnham, Ch8)
gloss_metaphors.The Net and the Submarines: — Zuboff: the surveillance net cast over a world of behaving objects to be captured
gloss_metaphors.The One-Way Mirror: — Zuboff: the asymmetry of knowledge and power; they see us, we cannot see them
gloss_metaphors.The Pattern in the Stream: — Kurzweil's whirlpool/stream figure of the self as persisting pattern (via Wiener, Heraclitus) (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_metaphors.The Pentecostal Company: — Handke's society of inspired not-doing; a community gathered in tiredness rather than performance (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_metaphors.The Planet at the Mirror Stage: — Ascott: the networked planet achieving self-recognition, as the infant before the mirror (via Lacan)
gloss_metaphors.The Private CIA and State Department: — the confabulating narrating self that spins a coherent story from incoherent experience (Harari)
gloss_metaphors.The Project as Projectile: — the self-as-project boomeranging back as self-aggression; freedom's collapse into auto-violence (Han, The Burnout Society)
gloss_metaphors.The Psibernetic Arch: — Ascott: the arch spanning the cybernetic and the psychic; the figure of psibernetics
gloss_metaphors.The Puppet Master Not the Puppet: — Zuboff: surveillance capitalism is the puppet master pulling the strings, not the puppet (the technology); the logic, not the tool
gloss_metaphors.The Salamander (Regeneration): — the monstrous, potent regrown limb as the figure of cyborg reconstitution after injury (Haraway)
gloss_metaphors.The Scent of Time (McLuhan): — incense-graduated time; the source text behind Han's Fragrance of Time (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Schmoo: — Al Capp's creature as the figure of automation's custom-on-demand abundance; the custom-built supplanting the mass-produced (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Screen of Transformations vs the Mir: — Ascott: the generative telematic screen against the passive reflecting mirror
gloss_metaphors.The Seamless Universal Web of Mystics an: — Ascott: the connective web that unites visionary states across mysticism and madness
gloss_metaphors.The Seamless Web: — the inclusive nonhierarchical decision-field the telephone introduces in place of the chain-of-command pyramid (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Second Body of a Million Molecular P: — Ascott: the distributed, luminous post-biological body of telepresence
gloss_metaphors.The Sorcerer's Apprentice: — Zuboff: the apparatus loosed beyond its makers' control
gloss_metaphors.The Soul as an Empty Carapace: — the soul as a dead metaphor; the disenchanted world as gears and levers (O'Gieblyn)
gloss_metaphors.The Technological Trojan Horse: — Zuboff: benign devices that smuggle the rendition apparatus into the home and the self
gloss_metaphors.The Telematic Embrace: — Ascott: the connective, loving embrace of the network (also a concept note)
gloss_metaphors.The Thermodynamics of an Artwork: — Ascott: the artwork as an energy/information system, read thermodynamically
gloss_metaphors.The Transistor Radio vs the Watch: — the visible-to-invisible shift in the scientific form-world (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.The Tribal Drum: — radio reaching the subliminal depths, turning psyche and society into 'a single echo chamber' (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.The Turing Test as a Magic Trick: — the test secures early assumptions so you 'become posthuman' before making any identification
gloss_metaphors.The Ultimate Tapeworm: — Zuboff: surveillance capitalism as a parasite embedded in the host of human life
gloss_metaphors.The Universe as a Spendthrift Artist: — Boulding via Burnham: the entropy of local order (Burnham)
gloss_metaphors.The Vampire That Feeds on Experience: — Zuboff: surveillance capitalism as a parasite living off the human experience it renders into data
gloss_metaphors.The Veil: — figure of negativity that makes beauty and eros possible
gloss_metaphors.The Web of Meaning: — intersubjective fictions trapping us like flies in a web of our own spinning (Harari)
gloss_metaphors.The Window vs the Doorway: — Ascott: the screen as a doorway to pass through, not a window to look through
gloss_metaphors.The Word as Virus: — Burroughs's figure (via Hayles): language as a literal corporeal parasite; the body materializes discourse
gloss_metaphors.The Wormhole: — Ascott: the figure of instantaneous passage between distant points/realities through the network
gloss_metaphors.To See in the Mind's Eye Is to Realize i: — Ascott: the constructive imagination that brings the envisioned into being
gloss_metaphors.Version 1.0 Biological Bodies: — the body as obsolescent hardware; the counter-figure to Hayles's 'data made flesh' (Kurzweil)
gloss_metaphors.We Are the Native Peoples Now: — Zuboff: conquest by declaration figured as the dispossession of indigenous peoples; we are the dispossessed
gloss_metaphors.We Wear All Mankind as Our Skin: — the global-village apex of the extension thesis (McLuhan)
gloss_metaphors.X Without Walls: — each electric medium as the abolition of an enclosure: speech / music hall / museum / space / classroom without walls (McLuhan)
